mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in over 55 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand across durable and non-durable sectors, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns during early-cycle phases, with a negative Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend levels confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 60-day equity returns, with a t-statistic below 1.5 across all historical samples.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of cases when CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.6 percent, contradicting the tightening fear narrative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, yielding positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal loses predictive accuracy, with 60-day forward returns turning negative in over 50 percent of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production and GDP growth indicates supply chain bottlenecks that constrain corporate output, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth falls below GDPC1 growth by more than 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with positive outcomes occurring in over 55 percent of triggered instances across rolling windows.
